mnemonics: I FEEL DECISIVE: IF-ELSE statements help your code make decisions, like choosing a path.
keyPoints: An if-else statement executes one block of code if a condition is True, and another block (the else) if it is False.
Nested if-else statements are if-else blocks placed inside another if or else block, allowing for more complex, multi-level decision making.
Proper indentation is critical in Python to define which code blocks belong to which conditional statement.
commonMistakes: Using a single equals sign (=) for comparison instead of a double equals sign (==). The first is for assignment, the second for checking equality.
Incorrect indentation, which causes code to execute in the wrong block or results in an IndentationError.
Forgetting the colon (:) at the end of the if, elif, and else statements.
